instruction: Edit the image so the blue resistance band is clearly under strong horizontal tension between the person’s two raised hands. Increase the hand separation so the band segment between the grips is about twice its relaxed length. The free span is taut and settles into a shallow, symmetric downward catenary curve rather than a straight line. Due to stretch, the band’s width is visibly reduced along the midspan (about 30–40%), while its blue color and satin/latex sheen are preserved; specular highlights run along the stretched length. At each grip, the band wraps flush around the fingers with a small bend radius set by its thickness, showing flattened contact patches and slight indentations in the skin; no gaps, twists, or overlaps at the grips. Update lighting and shadows to match the new geometry so the band casts a thin, consistent shadow on the fingers and nearby areas under the existing overhead light. Keep the brick wall background and the person’s pose otherwise unchanged. Depict the final, stable hold with no motion blur, the tightened band running horizontally across the upper portion of the frame.

prompts.superficial: Stretch the resistance band horizontally by pulling it apart with both hands.
prompts.intermediate: Stretch the blue resistance band horizontally between the two raised hands, increasing the distance between the grips. Treat the band as an elastic soft body anchored at the hands and account for gravity, tension, and contact with the skin. Ensure lighting and shadows are updated to follow the new geometry and maintain correct perspective, without introducing new elements.
prompts.explicit: Edit the image so the blue resistance band is clearly under strong horizontal tension between the person’s two raised hands. Increase the hand separation so the band segment between the grips is about twice its relaxed length. The free span is taut and settles into a shallow, symmetric downward catenary curve rather than a straight line. Due to stretch, the band’s width is visibly reduced along the midspan (about 30–40%), while its blue color and satin/latex sheen are preserved; specular highlights run along the stretched length. At each grip, the band wraps flush around the fingers with a small bend radius set by its thickness, showing flattened contact patches and slight indentations in the skin; no gaps, twists, or overlaps at the grips. Update lighting and shadows to match the new geometry so the band casts a thin, consistent shadow on the fingers and nearby areas under the existing overhead light. Keep the brick wall background and the person’s pose otherwise unchanged. Depict the final, stable hold with no motion blur, the tightened band running horizontally across the upper portion of the frame.

annotated_qa_pairs.question: Is a hand gripping the left end of the blue resistance band?
Is the hand gripping the right end of the blue resistance band?
Does the band between the hands form a symmetric downward catenary curve?
Does the band cast a shadow on both the fingers and the adjacent skin?
Is the resistance band horizontal between the two hands?
